An SSE connection that sends nothing while it waits for\n17\t# events is indistinguishable from a dead one: the browser's EventSource only\n18\t# fires ``onerror`` when there is I/O to fail, so a *silent* network drop on an\n19\t# idle stream leaves the surface showing \"Connected\" indefinitely (until the\n20\t# OS-level TCP keepalive times out, many minutes later). Emitting a comment\n21\t# line (``\": ping\\n\\n\"``) every few seconds keeps traffic flowing, so:\n22\t#\n23\t#   - proxies/load balancers won't reap the connection as idle, and\n24\t#   - the next ping after a real network drop fails to arrive, the client read\n25\t#     errors, ``onerror`` fires, and the native EventSource reconnects — which\n26\t#     is exactly the resync path the canvas relies on.\n27\t#\n28\t# Comment lines are ignored by EventSource (they never surface as messages), so\n29\t# this adds no client-visible events. ``5s`` is frequent enough that a drop is\n30\t# surfaced well inside any realistic reconnect budget, and quiet enough not to\n31\t# be chatty. Lower it (e.g. via ADC_SSE_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS) for faster\n32\t# reconnection tests.\n33\tKEEPALIVE_INTERVAL_SECONDS = float(\n34\t    os.environ.get(\"ADC_SSE_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS\", \"5\")\n35\t)\n36\t\n37\t# Sentinel pushed onto a connection's queue by :meth:`SSEBroadcaster.drop_session`\n38\t# to make that connection's :meth:`event_generator` return ABRUPTLY — i.e. without\n39\t# emitting a ``disconnect`` event. It defines a typed event\n6\tmodel (``SSEEventType`` + ``Event``) and an ``SSEManager`` that maintains a\n7\tregistry of caller-provided ``asyncio`` queues and broadcasts typed events to\n8\tthem.\n9\t\n10\tReal canvas surfaces connect through the SSE stream endpoint in ``src.main``,\n11\twhich registers them with the lower-level :class:`~src.sse.broadcaster.SSEBroadcaster`.\n12\t``broadcast_component_update`` therefore mirrors the event onto that shared\n13\tbroadcaster so live canvases update in place whenever a component is versioned.\n14\t\"\"\"\n15\t\n16\timport asyncio\n17\timport logging\n18\tfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\n19\tfrom enum import Enum\n20\tfrom typing import Dict, Optional\n21\tfrom uuid import uuid4\n22\t\n23\tfrom .broadcaster import SSEEvent, get_broadcaster\n24\t\n25\tlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n26\t\n27\t\n28\tclass SSEEventType(Enum):\n29\t    \"\"\"Typed SSE event names.\"\"\"\n30\t\n31\t    # Connection lifecycle\n32\t    CONNECTED = \"connected\"\n33\t    DISCONNECT = \"disconnect\"\n34\t\n35\t    # Result events\n36\t    RESULT_CREATED = \"result_created\"\n37\t    RESULT_UPDATED = \"result_updated\"\n38\t\n39\t    # Component events (Phase 2)\n40\t    COMPONENT_UPDATED = \"component_updated\"\n41\t\n42\t    # Topic events\n43\t    TOPIC_CREATED = \"topic_created\"\n44\t    TOPIC_UPDATED = \"topic_updated\"\n45\t\n46\t\n47\t@dataclass\n48\tclass Event:\n49\t    \"\"\"A typed SSE event delivered to a registered subscriber queue.\"\"\"\n50\t\n51\t    type: SSEEventType\n52\t    data: dict = field(default_factory=dict)\n53\t\n54\t\n55\tclass SSEManager:\n56\t    \"\"\"\n57\t    Manage SSE subscribers for the Phase 2 event loop.\n58\t\n59\t    Subscribers register an ``asyncio.Queue`` and receive typed :class:`Event`\n60\t    objects. Broadcasts are mirrored onto the global ``SSEBroadcaster`` so that\n61\t    canvas surfaces connected through the SSE stream endpoint receive the same\n62\t    events.\n63\t    \"\"\"\n64\t\n65\t    def __init__(self) -> None:\n66\t        self._subscribers: Dict[str, asyncio.Queue] = {}\n67\t\n68\t    async def register(self, queue: asyncio.Queue) -> str:\n69\t        \"\"\"Register a subscriber queue and return its subscription id.\"\"\"\n70\t        sub_id = str(uuid4())\n71\t        self._subscribers[sub_id] = queue\n72\t        logger.info(\"Registered SSE subscriber %s\", sub_id)\n73\t        return sub_id\n74\t\n75\t    async def unregister(self, sub_id: str) -> None:\n76\t        \"\"\"Remove a previously registered subscriber queue.\"\"\"\n77\t        if self._subscribers.pop(sub_id, None) is not None:\n78\t            logger.info(\"Unregistered SSE subscriber %s\", sub_id)\n79\t\n80\t    async def broadcast(self, event: Event) -> int:\n81\t        \"\"\"\n82\t        Push ``event`` onto every registered subscriber queue.\n83\t\n84\t        Returns the number of queues the event was delivered to.\n85\t        \"\"\"\n86\t        sent = 0\n87\t        for queue in list(self._subscribers.values()):\n88\t            try:\n89\t                queue.put_nowait(event)\n90\t                sent += 1\n91\t            except asyncio.QueueFull:\n92\t                logger.warning(\"Queue full for subscriber, dropping event\")\n93\t        return sent\n94\t\n95\t    async def broadcast_component_update(\n96\t        self,\n97\t        component_id: str,\n98\t        version: int,\n99\t        change_note: Optional[str] = None,\n100\t    ) -> int:\n101\t        \"\"\"\n102\t        Broadcast a ``component_updated`` event.\n103\t\n104\t        Delivered to every registered subscriber (the Phase 2 event loop) and\n105\t        mirrored onto the global ``SSEBroadcaster`` so live canvas connections\n106\t        update in place when a component is versioned. Returns the number of\n107\t        subscriber queues the event was delivered to.\n108\t        \"\"\"\n109\t        event = Event(\n110\t            type=SSEEventType.COMPONENT_UPDATED,\n111\t            data={\n112\t                \"component_id\": component_id,\n113\t                \"version\": version,\n114\t                \"change_note\": change_note,\n115\t            },\n116\t        )\n117\t\n118\t        sent = await self.broadcast(event)\n119\t\n120\t        # Mirror onto the broadcaster so real canvas surfaces receive the push.\n121\t        try:\n122\t            broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n123\t            await broadcaster.broadcast(\n124\t                SSEEvent(\n125\t                    event_type=SSEEventType.COMPONENT_UPDATED.value,\n126\t                    data={\n127\t                        \"component_id\": component_id,\n128\t                        \"version\": version,\n129\t                    },\n130\t                )\n131\t            )\n132\t        except Exception as exc:  # pragma: no cover - defensive\n133\t            logger.error(\"Failed to mirror component_updated to broadcaster: %s\", exc)\n134\t\n135\t        return sent\n136\t\n137\t\n138\t# Global SSE manager instance\n139\t_sse_manager: Optional[SSEManager] = None\n140\t\n141\t\n142\tdef get_sse_manager() -> SSEManager:\n143\t    \"\"\"Get or create the global SSE manager.\"\"\"\n144\t    global _sse_manager\n145\t    if _sse_manager is None:\n146\t        _sse_manager = SSEManager()\n147\t    return _sse_manager\n148\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_23bd23efc82144bbbfe3c0b2","session_id":"a11234b7-afdc-4d1e-88eb-d000aabec089","uuid":"05c07749-4b41-450d-98b8-933c8a220a6f","timestamp":"2026-07-23T15:06:38.649Z","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Explore SSE and component rendering"}

monitoring-originated rows: ``\"monitoring:{project_slug}\"``.\n55\t\n56\t    A missing project slug collapses to ``\"general\"`` so the key is always a\n57\t    non-empty, colon-segmented string the selector can index on.\n58\t    \"\"\"\n59\t    slug = project_slug or \"general\"\n60\t    itype = intent_type or \"status\"\n61\t    if itype == \"monitoring\":\n62\t        return f\"monitoring:{slug}\"\n63\t    if itype == \"lookup\" and lookup_kind:\n64\t        return f\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{slug}\"\n65\t    return f\"{itype}:{slug}\"\n66\t\n67\t\n68\t@dataclass\n69\tclass RenderOutcome:\n70\t    \"\"\"Result of a hot-path render: either a real component render, or a fallback flag.\n71\t\n72\t    ``rendered_html`` is None exactly when no component matched (the client must\n73\t    render the built-in generic fallback card). ``card_fallback`` is the boolean\n74\t    mirror persisted onto the result row so loadTopics()/the SSE consumer both\n75\t    see the same decision without re-running the selector.\n76\t    \"\"\"\n77\t\n78\t    rendered_html: Optional[str]\n79\t    component_id: Optional[str]\n80\t    card_fallback: bool\n81\t    layout_bucket: str\n82\t\n83\t\n84\tclass HotPathRenderer:\n85\t    \"\"\"Deterministic server-side card selector — never on the LLM path.\n86\t\n87\t    Selection is a single ``component_usage_patterns`` lookup on\n88\t    ``result_type``; on a match it fills the template (escaping every\n89\t    interpolated value — the render-path escaping boundary), writes\n90\t    ``card_cache``, and bumps the usage stats. On no match it returns a\n91\t    fallback outcome and writes nothing to the component DB.\n92\t    \"\"\"\n93\t\n94\t    def __init__(\n95\t        self,\n96\t        library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n97\t        match_threshold: float = DEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD,\n98\t        layout_bucket: str = DEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET,\n99\t    ):\n100\t        self.library = library or get_library()\n101\t        self.match_threshold = match_threshold\n102\t        self.layout_bucket = layout_bucket\n103\t\n104\t    def render(\n105\t        self,\n106\t        result_id: str,\n107\t        result_type: str,\n108\t        result_data: dict[str, Any],\n109\t        summary: Optional[str] = None,\n110\t        layout_bucket: Optional[str] = None,\n111\t    ) -> RenderOutcome:\n112\t        \"\"\"Select + render a card for one result. Deterministic, no LLM.\n113\t\n114\t        On a match: fills the template (escaped), writes card_cache keyed\n115\t        ``(result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)``, and records the usage\n116\t        pattern + usage stats. On no match / below threshold: returns a fallback\n117\t        outcome and writes nothing to the component DB (the client renders the\n118\t        built-in generic fallback card from result.data + summary).\n119\t        \"\"\"\n120\t        bucket = layout_bucket or self.layout_bucket\n121\t\n122\t        component = self.library.select_component_for_result_type(\n123\t            result_type, self.match_threshold\n124\t        )\n125\t        if component is None:\n126\t            logger.debug(\n127\t                \"hot-path fallback: no component matched result_type=%s \"\n128\t                \"(threshold %.2f) for result %s\",\n129\t                result_type,\n130\t                self.match_threshold,\n131\t                result_id,\n132\t            )\n133\t            return RenderOutcome(\n134\t                rendered_html=None,\n135\t                component_id=None,\n136\t                card_fallback=True,\n137\t                layout_bucket=bucket,\n138\t            )\n139\t\n140\t        rendered_html = fill_template(component.html_template, result_data)\n141\t\n142\t        # The server is the sole writer of card_cache + usage stats (write-scope\n143\t        # separation). cache_card bumps components.usage_count/last_used;\n144\t        # record_usage_pattern updates component_usage_patterns (sample_count,\n145\t        # last_matched, running match_score). Neither touches component\n146\t        # definitions — that is the UI-regen agent's job.\n147\t        self.library.cache_card(\n148\t            result_id, component.id, component.version, bucket, rendered_html\n149\t        )\n150\t        # A hot-path match is a confirmed, high-confidence usage — record at 1.0\n151\t        # so reliably-matching components trend toward the top of the selector.\n152\t        self.library.record_usage_pattern(component.id, result_type, match_score=1.0)\n153\t\n154\t        logger.info(\n155\t            \"hot-path rendered result %s with component %s v%s (result_type=%s)\",\n156\t            result_id,\n157\t            component.id,\n158\t            component.version,\n159\t            result_type,\n160\t        )\n161\t        return RenderOutcome(\n162\t            rendered_html=rendered_html,\n163\t            component_id=component.id,\n164\t            card_fallback=False,\n165\t            layout_bucket=bucket,\n166\t        )\n167\t\n168\t\n169\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n170\t# Template fill — the render-path escaping boundary\n171\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n172\t\n173\tdef fill_template(template: str, result_data: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n174\t    \"\"\"Flat dot-path substitution with HTML escaping at fill time.\n175\t\n176\t    Every ``{{field.path}}`` token is replaced with the HTML-escaped value at\n177\t    that dot-path (list indices via numeric segments, e.g. ``{{pods.0.name}}``).\n178\t    Unknown paths resolve to \"\". This is the escaping boundary the plan binds\n179\t    every server-filled card to (UI-Regen Agent → \"Escaping contract\"): a\n180\t    markup-looking log line interpolated here renders as literal text instead of\n181\t    breaking layout or executing in the canvas. No loops or conditionals — the\n182\t    generator is instructed to emit only flat placeholders.\n183\t    \"\"\"\n184\t    if not template:\n185\t        return \"\"\n186\t\n187\t    def replace_value(match: re.Match) -> str:\n188\t        path = match.group(1).strip()\n189\t        value = _get_nested_value(result_data, path)\n190\t        if value is None:\n191\t            return \"\"\n192\t        return html.escape(str(value), quote=False)\n193\t\n194\t    return _PLACEHOLDER.sub(replace_value, template)\n195\t\n196\t\n197\tdef _get_nested_value(data: dict[str, Any], path: str) -> Any:\n198\t    \"\"\"Resolve a dot-path against a dict/list (numeric segments index lists).\"\"\"\n199\t    value: Any = data\n200\t    for key in path.split(\".\"):\n201\t        if isinstance(value, dict):\n202\t            value = value.get(key)\n203\t        elif isinstance(value, list) and key.isdigit() and int(key) < len(value):\n204\t            value = value[int(key)]\n205\t        else:\n206\t            return None\n207\t    return value\n208\t\n209\t\n210\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n211\t# Singleton\n212\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n213\t\n214\t_renderer: Optional[HotPathRenderer] = None\n215\t\n216\t\n217\tdef get_renderer(\n218\t ``library=`` (and ``reset=True``) to rebind it — how tests point the\n224\t    renderer at an isolated component DB without touching the production\n225\t    ``data/components.db`` singleton.\n226\t    \"\"\"\n227\t    global _renderer\n228\t    if _renderer is None or reset or library is not None:\n229\t        _renderer = HotPathRenderer(library=library)\n230\t    return Dataset attributes are set for querying (component_id, topic_id, topic_type)\n4. Staleness indicators render correctly\n5. Topic metadata (label, type) renders in the header\n\nThese tests ensure the hot-path renderer's HTML output streams to SSE and\ninjects into the canvas correctly (no blank canvas on a component \"\"\"Collect SSE events until all wanted types arrive (excluding 'connected').\"\"\"\n    wanted = set(wanted_types)\n    collected = []\n\n    async def drain():\n        async for wire in broadcaster.event_generator(conn):\n            for etype, data in parse_sse_stream(wire):\n                if etype == \"connected\":\n                    continue\n                collected.append((etype, data))\n                if wanted <= {e for e, _ in collected}:\n                    return\n\n    task = asyncio.create_task(drain())\n    try:\n        await asyncio.wait_for(task, timeout=timeout)\n    except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n        task.cancel()\n        raise\n    return collected\n\n\n# --- Component path tests (seeded match) renders fallback via SSE (first-ever shape).\"\"\"\n\n    async def test_no_component_match_includes_card_fallback_in_sse(\n        self, isolated_store\n    ):\n        \"\"\"When no component matches, card_fallback=True is in SSE.\"\"\"\n        store = isolated_store\n\n        # Create a session and surface\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        surface_id = await           b.unregister(conn.connection_id)\n\n    async def test_fallback_card_renders_from_sse_event(\n        self, isolated_store\n    ):\n        \"\"\"Canvas renders fallback card correctly from SSE event data.\"\"\"\n        # Simulate the card data structure for fallback\n        card_data = {\n            \"topic\": {\n                \"id\": \"t-fallback\",\n                \"label\":
